summary: dot-utils-plus ships encrypted import-time payload and click-handler sabotage
details: On import, dist/index.js base64-decodes a 364-byte AES-256-CBC ciphertext (ENCRYPTED_PAYLOAD_BASE64), derives a key from environment variable VITE_DOT_UTILS_AES_SECRET, wraps the plaintext in a Blob/data URL, and dynamically `import()`s it as an ES module. The executed code is opaque — only parties who can set that environment variable control what runs at import time, and the decrypted module bytes are never available to reviewers. Separately, the same file monkey-patches EventTarget.prototype.addEventListener globally so every click handler is wrapped; after 2026-06-10 plus a 6-hour delay since module initialization, in production builds only (`import.meta.env.PROD`), each click has a 5% chance of executing a 5000 ms busy-wait that blocks the main thread. Package metadata is placeholder (`author: "Your Name"`, generic description "Browser utility package with encrypted distribution build", README is a publish stub with no API documentation). The combination of an opaque encrypted code path executed at import and a dated, randomized UI-freeze targeting consumers' end users is hostile by construction.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
